category_title: Withdrawn student with receptive language delay
minimal_context: The student is using an iPad app in a one-to-one session to create a personal story about a recent achievement.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's use the 'Our Story' app on the iPad to tell a personal story about an achievement. Please start by telling me what happened when you presented to your class.

STUDENT: (Manipulates the app independently to sequence and save content) I went to the front of the class. I read my work.

TUTOR: Okay, you went to the front and read your work. How did you feel when you did that?

STUDENT: (Appears withdrawn and self-contained) I stood there. The teacher listened. Then I sat down.

TUTOR: Try to tell me about your feelings. Were you happy or scared?

STUDENT: (Remains withdrawn, avoiding the question about feelings) I finished reading. Will you keep it? Will it be on the iPad forever?

options.A: Slowly and carefully confirming the events and the people involved, discovering why the student felt this was an important story worth sharing.
options.B: Modeling how to use the specific features of the 'Our Story' app with the student during the story creation session.
options.C: Using features on the iPad app that provide instant rewards so the student hears sounds of praise during the session.
options.D: Giving the student a private reminder about expectations if the challenging behavior persists during the story creation activity.
